constraints: Do not treat sample PCI addresses, interface names, IB device names, representor names, queue counts, or NUMA placement as current-machine facts.
Do not mix or upgrade individual DOCA packages independently unless local package metadata documents that combination.
Ask for read-only environment evidence before generating code that depends on host, BlueField, NIC, GPU, PCIe, NUMA, representor, queue, or memory-domain topology.
devices: The SDK source exposes DOCA device, devinfo, representor, mmap, buffer, and package-facing sample patterns; runtime device facts require local read-only discovery.
Agents should model host, BlueField Arm-side, NIC, GPU, PCIe, NUMA, representor, queue, and memory-domain observations separately before selecting topology-specific code.
Example selectors such as PCI address, interface name, IB device name, mlx name, GPU ID, and representor ID are inputs to discover or validate, not stable defaults.
libraries: Top-level Meson files, SDK headers, package metadata, and sample/application dependencies enumerate the visible SDK library surface.
Common SDK facilities provide device, version, logging, mmap, buffer, and utility contracts used by samples and applications.
This repository-level contract covers SDK-level source, package, sample, and application facts.
modes: Repository, package, sample, application, and SDK-header views can differ; use source_globs and package manifests to determine the current source view.
Build planning and source edits are local-source actions, while runtime sample execution, device mode changes, firmware actions, and persistent configuration are separate approval classes.
Host-side, BlueField-side, switch, queue, memory-domain, and offload modes must be discovered through local sensors or framework-specific capability checks.
offloads: The repository-level SDK contract does not prove any runtime offload is available on the current machine.
Use package-facing module documentation or package-provided source evidence before planning Flow steering, DPA execution, PCC behavior, RDMA, DMA, GPU, crypto, or other offload families.
Agent-produced code should call capability or support checks before enabling device-specific offload paths.
versions: Read VERSION, top-level meson.build, package metadata, and SDK doca_version headers before making source-package version claims.
Use compatibility tests, SDK headers, and release-facing package data before making API-stability or package-combination claims.
Treat installed package, driver, firmware, DPDK, and source-package versions as runtime facts to collect when they affect agent-produced code.

blocked_by_default: Driver reloads, firmware changes, or sysfs/devlink writes
Package installation, hugepage changes, or persistent host configuration
Device, network, credential, or runtime state mutation
Package installation
Device, network, credential, or persistent configuration changes
Runtime sample execution
needs: Topology facts with measured or not_measured status

blocked_by_default: Package installation
System path, driver, hugepage, or persistent host configuration changes
Device, network, credential, or production state changes
needs: Installed header or prefix evidence when source helpers are absent

blocked_by_default: Invented APIs or dependency names
Runtime offload claims without local evidence
Source edits before API and dependency evidence is collected
needs: Samples or applications that exercise the APIs

blocked_by_default: Runtime sample execution
Package installation
Device, network, traffic, or persistent configuration changes
Service startup
Live traffic
Device, network, firmware, credential, or persistent configuration changes
Commands that write build output without approval
Device, network, credential, or runtime state mutation
Undocumented flags or output paths

capabilities.summary: Repository-level SDK source, build, package, and SDK-header behavior for DOCA coding agents.
